item.title: Taipan Bite - A Tragedy in Three Acts
item.description:
The Greek tragedy is a dark comedy wherein the protagonist suffers due to their own inherent character flaws. In our play, arrogance. This guy regularly went on hate-filled, misogynistic, racist, typo-ridden rants against anyone who disagreed with his practices or offered even a little criticism that could have prevented this outcome.
Inland Taipans are, drop-for-drop, the most venomous snake in the world. Despite their potency, they have never killed anyone. Cue South Carolina man. In case it needed any clarification, using bare hands to work with venomous snakes - called 'freehandling' - is inherently a horribly dangerous practice. Reptiles and mammals don't communicate in the same ways, and we misunderstand each others' behaviors all the time. No snake wants to bite a human maliciously, despite myths to the contrary. But any animal will defend themselves if they believe their life is at risk. Everything this man is saying in the video is incorrect - this young snake could bite him at any moment if it wanted to. All snakes want to do is hide or get away if possible, as this taipan is showing.
Begging for antivenom a few hours after the handling video was posted. As a result of freehandling the world's most venomous snake without even having access to antivenom, this man may lose his life. All of his animals have been euthanized, and he's drawn a ton of negative attention to these already misunderstood species. Here's the latest well-written piece from the media:
If you want to own venomous snakes, fine. But get formal training. Take precautions. Use tools. Have antivenom. Don't get one to be cool - no one will think you are. Don't get one for clicks or views on social media - you're likely to be maimed or killed. Don't get them on impulse - it takes a long time to plan and make connections for emergency response plans. And actually take care of them with the same love you have for your family dog or cat. The conditions his animals were kept in were apparently horrific. *Not celebrating a guy fighting for his life, but this serves as a stark warning against these unsafe practices. It also couldn't have happened to a better guy /s
item.dc:creator: ecologistguy

item.title: Got dressed up and did my makeup to go see Beetlejuice (2024)
item.description: My skeleton and muscle/fat distributions don't really let me pass easily as a lady. I've had a man's body all my life and I'm mostly bothered by what I don't have than what I do. I've been working on presenting as a woman for a bit and only recently got over some karenphobia to start using the ladies' room again. Strangers go both ways in deciding what I am and I'm comfortable enough in my femininity that their uncertainty doesn't bother me. Unless it's malicious. Fortunately I'm still scary enough I can convince people to treat me as the lady I am, rather than the thing they see. (I'm getting old, so that probably won't work much longer.) But today, I felt like I looked pretty. I am of an age that to have seen all of Burton's movies at the cinema on release. Beetlejuice (1988) was not foundational for the baby bat that I was, but it was certainly formative. So I was fondly looking forward to the unexpected sequel. Enough that I got my nails done, pulled out a lacy number and did the little makeup I feel confident (skill-wise) in applying. I even have an appropriate parasol, but it wasn't sunny enough or rainy enough to warrant it. So I got out the hat I've been wearing that doesn't really fit the mood, but is fine. And when I looked at myself in the mirror, for the first time ever, I thought I looked pretty. And that was nice. FP edit: Thank you for the kind intentions behind most of your words.
item.dc:creator: xizar

item.title: That’s a no from me, dawg
item.description: My contract is ending, but they want me to stay on another contract Currently work from home, M-F 830-5 New contract —5% pay cut (non-negotiable). Some shifts start at 5:30am and Can go as late as 10:30 pm. Must work 1 day of the weekend every week. At least 2 days a week I must be in the office My wife said ‘fuck that, be a house husband and make me sandwiches’

item.title: The last stones I'll probably ever restore.
item.description:
I spent the last couple years volunteering my services at Belle Passi Cemetery in Woodburn Oregon. I spent thousands of hours, thousands of dollars, restored over 600 stones, and I asked nothing in return.
I always research the people who I work on. I got to know hundreds of the personal histories of the families in the area and the people buried there.
I cared very much about the work I was doing. I invested every extra dollar and minute I had. I even put on events and brought in volunteers. I gave them my work as content for their website and Facebook. I found friends to help with working in the office.
Sometimes you find out that your friend behaves in a way you can't sanction. My friend couldn't accept that I had issues with their behavior. Instead of moving on, the friend told the cemetery lies about me. Without asking me about it, they believed the lies. When that friend and now cemetery employees husband assaulted me, the cemetery asked me for my keys. The insult is pretty significant and I won't be returning. I don't think they even feel like they did anything wrong. And the work I've done becomes a memory that makes me cry. And doing the work reminds me of sad things now. So I think I'm probably going to have to be done now. I'm sorry. And thank you.
I am trained to preserve and restore stone. Please do not attempt this without training or guidance from someone who knows what they are doing. This is not as simple as it seems, and it is very easy to permanently damage the stones. If you would like to find out how to participate or learn, contact your local historical society and ask them about programs and groups where you can learn. If you would like to help clean cemeteries in other ways, please see my friend Olives post: https://imgur.com/gallery/N27pVMj I do this as a hobby, I do not get paid. If you want to help provide materials, here is my link- https://ko-fi.com/springchikun
item.dc:creator: Springchikun
